title: QA: ASCII-only lowercase in denied_reason can be bypassed via non-ASCII
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/tools/bash/policy.rs:89-136
## Violation
`denied_reason` lowercases via `command.to_ascii_lowercase()` (policy.rs:123). Non-ASCII bytes pass unchanged. Combined with bash's tolerance of UTF-8, this is a fragile foundation; more importantly the code is deceptively named ("case-insensitive" denial but only handles ASCII case).

Quoted (policy.rs:121-130):
```
    let lower = command.to_ascii_lowercase();

    for (pat, reason) in SUBSTRINGS {
        if lower.contains(pat) {
            return Some(reason);
        }
    }
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Either fold full Unicode (use `unicode-case-mapping`) and document, OR
2. Document the denylist is ASCII-only and reject any non-ASCII byte up-front in strict mode.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
